Tracing the inference chain from reported statistics to stated conclusion is therefore load-bearing for this delegation, making logical consistency a central responsibility.","R1.3":"This is the direct object of the delegation. Every specific claim must be cross-checked against the reported numbers: (i) η_0.15 improvement (3.1% vs 4.7%) is directionally consistent; (ii) σ_NMAD improvement (0.021 vs 0.027) is consistent; (iii) the '~30% improvement' gloss is ambiguous—it matches the outlier-fraction reduction (~34%) but not the σ_NMAD reduction (~22%), and the text applies it broadly to 'calibration' without tying it to one metric; (iv) the PIT evidence (W² = 0.084 vs 0.031) actually contradicts the calibration claim, constituting a critical evidence–claim misalignment that invalidates the delegation if uncorrected."},"clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":""}

rationale.R1.2: The PIT paragraph contains an internal logical inversion: a higher Cramér–von Mises W² indicates greater deviation from uniformity (worse calibration), yet the text labels FlowPhot's W² = 0.084 'favorable' relative to the baseline's W² = 0.031. Tracing the inference chain from reported statistics to stated conclusion is therefore load-bearing for this delegation, making logical consistency a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: This is the direct object of the delegation. Every specific claim must be cross-checked against the reported numbers: (i) η_0.15 improvement (3.1% vs 4.7%) is directionally consistent; (ii) σ_NMAD improvement (0.021 vs 0.027) is consistent; (iii) the '~30% improvement' gloss is ambiguous—it matches the outlier-fraction reduction (~34%) but not the σ_NMAD reduction (~22%), and the text applies it broadly to 'calibration' without tying it to one metric; (iv) the PIT evidence (W² = 0.084 vs 0.031) actually contradicts the calibration claim, constituting a critical evidence–claim misalignment that invalidates the delegation if uncorrected.
